models.generation_context: Modern Integra revived for 2023 MY as a premium sport compact 4-door liftback based on the Honda Civic Si platform. 2026 is a mid-cycle refresh adding three new exterior colors, an A-Spec aero body kit, and a larger 9-inch standard touchscreen on lower trims.
models.model_summary: Acura's entry-luxury compact, a 4-door liftback positioned above the Civic Si with a 1.5L turbo I4 and CVT (the A-Spec Technology adds a 6-speed manual option). The 2026 refresh updates exterior styling and the infotainment suite. The Integra Type S is marketed as a separate model.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 model-specific VDS score (VDS measures 3-year-old vehicles). The current-gen Integra (on sale since 2023) shares its platform and powertrain with the Honda Civic, which has a strong long-term reliability history.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Acura's brand-level APEAL has historically tracked near the premium-segment average. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Integra for its premium-feeling interior, composed chassis, and the unusual-for-the-class availability of a 6-speed manual (on the A-Spec Technology Package). Common criticisms are that the 200 hp 1.5L turbo can feel modest at this price point and that the Integra commands a notable premium over the Civic Si it is based on. The 2026 refresh adds tech and styling tweaks but no powertrain changes.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds and KBB owner-review pages for the 2026 Integra are sparse this early in the model year. Carryover sentiment from 2023-2025 owners is generally positive on build quality and dynamics, with criticisms centered on cargo loading height and a soft 1.5L powerband.

notable_other: 9-inch touchscreen (new for 2026 — was 7-inch on lower trims previously)

trims.notes: Base trim of the Integra (FWD ICE powertrain line). 2026 refresh increased the standard infotainment screen to 9 inches across the lineup and added a power moonroof and wireless CarPlay/Android Auto. 0-60 is an enthusiast-press estimate; Acura does not publish 0-60 for the 1.5T configuration. IIHS rating year is 2025 (carried over per IIHS Acura page listing the Integra 4-door hatchback). Manufacturer primary source 403 to WebFetch; values cross-validated against multiple secondary sources during initial research. Source citation for msrp_base/destination_fee updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.acura.com/integra); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

Wider 235/40R18 tires
LED fog lights and black exterior trim
A-Spec sport pedals, steering wheel, badging

trims.notes: A-Spec is an appearance package on the 1.5T CVT — same powertrain as the base Integra, restyled. EPA mpg figures carry over from base. Manufacturer primary source 403 to WebFetch; values cross-validated against multiple secondary sources during initial research. Source citation for msrp_base/destination_fee updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.acura.com/integra); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

Shares image trim family with A-Spec for the exterior look package, but adds tech/audio differentiation inside. The manual transmission is the enthusiast pick — Acura's only manual offering in the lineup outside the Type S. Manufacturer primary source 403 to WebFetch; values cross-validated against multiple secondary sources during initial research. Source citation for msrp_base/destination_fee updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.acura.com/integra); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

models.notes: Integra Type S is treated as a separate model per the performance-variant rule (Acura markets it on its own product line, fueleconomy.gov groups it under Integra by engine but Acura's marketing and editorial coverage treats it distinctly). 2026 is a mid-cycle refresh: new front fascia styling cues, three new colors, A-Spec aero body kit, larger standard 9-inch touchscreen, additional standard tech. IIHS classifies the Integra under '4-door hatchback' confirming the liftback body style.

models.generation_context: High-performance variant of the modern Integra introduced for 2024 MY. Shares its 2.0L K20C1 turbo I4 and 6-speed manual with the Honda Civic Type R, tuned for Acura with adaptive damping and Brembo brakes. 2026 carries over with minor color/equipment updates.
models.model_summary: Sole-trim performance flagship of the Integra family: 320 hp 2.0L turbo, 6-speed manual only, FWD with helical limited-slip diff and adaptive dampers. Marketed as its own model by Acura and treated as such here.

reliability.summary: No 2026 model-specific reliability data published. Shares its K20C1 powertrain with the Honda Civic Type R, which has a strong durability record though early Type S/Type R owners have reported occasional fuel pump and infotainment issues.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a model-specific APEAL score. Enthusiast press response remains strongly positive; the Integra Type S earned the 2026 Car and Driver Editors' Choice award.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Type S as one of the best front-drive performance cars on sale: sharp turn-in, communicative manual gearbox, fade-resistant Brembo brakes, and a more refined cabin than the Civic Type R it is based on. Common criticisms are the steep premium over the Civic Type R, FWD-only configuration at a $53k+ price, and limited rear-seat headroom. Car and Driver named it an Editors' Choice for 2026.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner-review sample sizes for the 2026 MY Type S are too small to summarize meaningfully. Carryover 2024-2025 owners on Edmunds rate the car highly for driving feel; common complaints include road noise, ride harshness on broken pavement, and limited production allocation.

notable_other: Adaptive Damper System with Sport+ mode
Active Sound Control with adaptive sport exhaust (triple center exit)
Wider front and rear track and flared fenders

trims.notes: Sole trim of the Integra Type S model line (atomic sole-trim rule applied: is_base_trim true, delta_from_base null). NHTSA and IIHS do not separately rate the Type S; the IIHS '4-door hatchback' rating covers the Integra body style but the Type S has different curb weight and structural reinforcements so we leave Type S safety ratings null. Power output is manufacturer's combined-figure crank rating from the K20C1 turbo. Annual fuel cost from EPA fueleconomy.gov for id=49308 (premium gasoline). Cargo volume of 24.3 cu ft and 4-passenger seating (rear-bench delete vs the standard Integra's 5) per spec sheets — Type S has a slim 4-seat layout. Manufacturer primary source 403 to WebFetch; values cross-validated against multiple secondary sources during initial research.

models.notes: Separated from the Integra parent model per the performance-variant rule. Acura's marketing treats it as a distinct line, fueleconomy.gov gives it its own EPA entry, and Acura News publishes Type S-specific press releases. 2026 Type S was named a Car and Driver Editors' Choice.

models.generation_context: All-new for 2025 MY, the ADX is Acura's gateway compact crossover, slotting below the RDX. Shares its platform with the Honda HR-V/CR-V architecture. 2026 is its second model year with minor changes — primarily a new exterior color (Double Apex Blue Pearl II).
models.model_summary: Acura's smallest SUV, two-row, five-seat with a 1.5L turbo I4 (190 hp) and CVT. FWD standard; AWD a $2,000 option on every trim. Aimed at compact-luxury entry buyers cross-shopping the Audi Q3, BMW X1, and Volvo XC40.

reliability.summary: Too new to have JD Power VDS data (VDS measures 3-year-old vehicles; the ADX launched for 2025). The shared 1.5L turbo and CVT platform has a strong history in Honda HR-V/Civic applications.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The ADX is too new (launched for 2025) to have established owner-survey data. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the ADX for its long standard-feature list (heated front seats, power moonroof, power liftgate from the base trim), composed ride, and Acura-style interior quality at a $35k entry price. Common criticisms are that the 190 hp 1.5L turbo and CVT combination feels strained when loaded, and that the ADX is mechanically close to the cheaper Honda HR-V/CR-V it shares architecture with.

owner_reviews.summary: Limited 2026 MY owner reviews available. 2025 owners on Edmunds/KBB rate the ADX favorably on cabin quality and standard equipment; common complaints involve real-world fuel economy below the EPA window-sticker numbers and the 1.5T's modest passing power.

trims.notes: Base trim of the ADX FWD ICE powertrain line. AWD is a $2,000 option on every ADX trim; AWD entries (FWD-base + AWD, etc.) carry separate EPA ratings (28/26/30 combined for AWD vs 29/27/32 for FWD). Destination fee of $1,350 per cars.com 2026 listings; one secondary source cited $1,450 but the lower figure aligns with the recent press release. 0-60 estimated; Acura does not publish 0-60 for the ADX. Premium fuel is recommended (not required) per Acura. Manufacturer primary source 403 to WebFetch; dimensions cross-validated against multiple secondary sources during initial research. Source citation for msrp_base/destination_fee updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.acura.com/adx); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

notable_other: A-Spec aero body kit and front fascia

models.notes: ADX is new for 2025 and is Acura's gateway compact SUV. 2026 changes are minimal — adds Double Apex Blue Pearl II as a new color, retains $35,000 base price. AWD trims are not modeled as separate trim_slugs here; per master spec the AWD option adds $2,000 across the board, but Acura sells it as an option rather than a separate model code. EPA-tested AWD numbers (id=50074) and FWD numbers (id=50075) both verified.

models.generation_context: 3rd-gen RDX (TC1/TC2), on sale since 2019 MY with mid-cycle refresh for 2022. 2026 carries the same powertrain (2.0L turbo I4, 10-speed automatic, standard SH-AWD) with packaging refinements.
models.model_summary: Acura's volume-leading luxury compact SUV: every 2026 RDX comes with a 272 hp 2.0L turbo I4, a 10-speed automatic, and standard Super-Handling All-Wheel Drive (no FWD option). Five trims add equipment from base through A-Spec Advance.

reliability.summary: No 2026 model-specific VDS published. The 3rd-gen RDX (2019-present) has historically scored mid-pack in long-term reliability surveys, with the 10-speed automatic and infotainment as the most-cited friction points.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). JD Power's most recent Acura-brand APEAL scores have tracked above the industry average. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the RDX's punchy 272 hp turbo, standard SH-AWD's cornering grip, generous standard feature list, and competitive price among compact luxury SUVs. Common criticisms target the True Touchpad Interface infotainment (controversial since launch), aging cabin design relative to newer competitors like the Volvo XC60 and Genesis GV70, and a 21 mpg combined that lags class-leading hybrid competitors.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners on Edmunds and KBB rate the RDX highly for driving dynamics, build quality, and the ELS audio system on upper trims. Most-cited complaints are the True Touchpad infotainment learning curve and middling fuel economy.

trims.notes: Sole powertrain line for the RDX (every trim is SH-AWD with the 2.0T). IIHS Acura 2025-2026 awards do not list the RDX (Integra, ADX, MDX won; RDX did not), hence null. Towing capacity per Acura is 1,500 lb. Manufacturer primary source 403 to WebFetch; dimensions cross-validated against multiple secondary sources during initial research. Source citation for msrp_base/destination_fee updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.acura.com/rdx); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

models.notes: All RDX trims use the same powertrain (single powertrain line), so only the base trim is is_base_trim=true. 2026 RDX did not appear in the 2025-2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick list — only Integra, ADX, and MDX received TSP this round.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen MDX, on sale since 2022 MY (YD4). Mid-cycle refresh for 2025 added an updated front fascia, revised cabin tech, and the larger 12.3-inch touchscreen. 2026 carries the refresh forward with minor packaging changes.
models.model_summary: Acura's 3-row family flagship: 290 hp 3.5L V6, 10-speed automatic, FWD on base/Tech trims, available Super-Handling AWD (standard on A-Spec and above). Seven-seat 2-3-2 layout. The high-performance MDX Type S is treated as a separate model.

reliability.summary: No published 2026 model-specific VDS score. The 4th-gen MDX (2022-present) has had a couple of recalls (early-build infotainment, fuel pump) but generally rates average-to-above-average for long-term reliability.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Brand-level APEAL for Acura has historically tracked above industry average. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the MDX's quiet, well-built cabin, strong V6 powertrain, generous third-row access, and competitive base price among 3-row luxury SUVs. Common criticisms target the controversial True Touchpad infotainment (despite the 12.3-inch screen), tight third row vs class leaders like the Kia Telluride, and the lack of any hybrid option for the 2026 lineup.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners rate the MDX highly for build quality, ride refinement, and value vs class competition. Common complaints include the True Touchpad interface and middling cargo room behind the third row.

notable_other: Wi-Fi hotspot and over-the-air software updates

trims.notes: Base MDX trim, FWD with the 3.5L NA V6. Tow rating is 3,500 lb on FWD MDX (5,000 lb on SH-AWD trims per Acura). Source citation for msrp_base/destination_fee updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.acura.com/mdx); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

models.notes: MDX has a single powertrain line for the standard model (3.5L V6 NA). FWD and SH-AWD are modeled as separate trims for the base and Tech levels per the master spec AWD precedent; A-Spec/Advance/A-Spec Advance are SH-AWD only. The MDX Type S Advance is treated as its own model per the performance-variant rule (separate engine, separate Acura marketing, separate fueleconomy.gov entry id=49560).

models.generation_context: High-performance variant of the 4th-gen MDX, introduced for 2022 MY. 2026 carries forward with the 355 hp turbocharged 3.0L V6, adaptive air suspension, Brembo brakes, and exclusive 21-inch wheels. Available only as the fully-loaded Type S Advance.
models.model_summary: Sole-trim performance-luxury 3-row SUV: 355 hp / 354 lb-ft 3.0L turbo V6, standard SH-AWD, adaptive air suspension with adjustable ride height, Brembo front brakes, 21-inch wheels, ELS Studio 3D 31-speaker premium audio.

reliability.summary: No published 2026 model-specific VDS score. The Type S has fewer total units in the field than the standard MDX, so long-term data is sparse; early-production Type S models had some air-suspension and infotainment recalls that have since been addressed.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The MDX Type S is low-volume and may fall outside JD Power's statistically significant sample threshold even when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Type S as the most engaging 3-row luxury SUV in its price band: muscular turbo V6, adaptive air suspension that delivers both comfort and sharp body control, and a Sport+ mode that genuinely transforms the chassis. Common criticisms include a $75k+ price that puts it close to true performance SUVs from BMW and Porsche, the persistent True Touchpad infotainment frustration, and EPA combined mpg of 19.

owner_reviews.summary: Type S owners on Edmunds praise the engine response and air-suspension comfort/sport balance. Common complaints involve fuel economy, premium-fuel cost, and tire wear from the wide 275/40R21 setup.

notable_other: Adaptive air suspension with 4-corner self-leveling and adjustable ride height (6.7-9.4 in)
Active Sound Control with sport exhaust and rev-matching
ELS Studio 3D 31-speaker Signature Edition premium audio (top tier)
Massaging Nappa leather 16-way power front seats
Standard rear automatic braking added on Type S vs other MDX trims

trims.notes: Sole trim of the MDX Type S model line (atomic sole-trim rule applied: is_base_trim true, delta_from_base null). The 2026 Type S is sold only as 'Type S Advance' (Acura simplified the lineup vs prior years' two Type S configurations). Adaptive air suspension provides 6.7-9.4 in adjustable ride height; the curb-weight figure of 4,776 lb is from Acura specs. IIHS TSP covers the 'MDX 4-door SUV' which includes Type S body structure (same crashworthiness as standard MDX). Tire spec 275/40R21 is unique to Type S. Manufacturer primary source 403 to WebFetch; values cross-validated against multiple secondary sources during initial research. Source citation for msrp_base/destination_fee updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.acura.com/mdx-type-s); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

models.notes: MDX Type S treated as separate model per performance-variant rule (different engine, separate marketing on Acura's website 'MDX & MDX Type S', dedicated Edmunds 2026 page, separate fueleconomy.gov ID 49560). Single trim, both base-trim and delta-from-base set per the sole-trim atomic rule.
